verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 (TGN46) is a type I single-pass TM glycoprotein whose steady-state localization is the trans-Golgi network, but it constitutively cycles between the TGN and the plasma membrane via endosomes. It is packaged into CARTS (PKD-dependent TGN-to-PM carriers) and travels to the cell surface, where its luminal domain faces the extracellular space before a cytosolic YQRL tyrosine-based motif drives rapid endocytic retrieval back to the TGN. Multiple independent studies confirm a real, transient PM pool (surface biotinylation, colocalization with viral particles at the cell surface, flow-cytometry-accessible ectodomain). The dominant compartment is the TGN/endomembrane system, so `yes` is not warranted, but the well-documented constitutive cycling to the PM qualifies as `dual_localization`. No narrow lineage restriction, no stress/death induction, and no lysosomal exocytosis mechanism are required — the cycling is constitutive.
